Data musings

Notes from building production data platforms — engineering, governance, experimentation, and the metrics behind product decisions.

23 May 2026 Product / Strategy
Beyond A/B Tests: Using Cohort Analysis to Model Product Commitment Before You Build It When the cost of a wrong product bet is high, cohort analysis of past behaviour is more useful than a speculative experiment. Here's how retention curves moved a K4 number from 26% to 48% — before a single engineer was tasked.
Cohort AnalysisRetentionForecastingProduct
15 Apr 2026 Segmentation
The SFF Ratio: Telling Abuse from Operational Failure with Segmentation A spike in Self-Force-Finish events looked like abuse. Segmentation revealed it was high-value customers signalling a GPS black hole — and the fix belonged in product, not policy.
SegmentationUser BehaviourOperationsProduct
10 Mar 2026 BI / Tooling
Looker vs. Power BI: Aligning BI Tools to How Fast Stakeholders Need to Decide A single BI tool serving all stakeholders is a governance failure in waiting. The right frame is decision latency — how fast each audience actually needs to act on what they see.
LookerPower BIBI StrategyGovernance
18 Feb 2026 Engineering / Platform
From Zero to KPI: Architecting a BigQuery Warehouse for IoT Telemetry The architectural decisions behind standing up a production-grade GCP warehouse that handles high-velocity IoT streams and still answers an exec's question in seconds.
BigQueryIoTAirflowGCP
27 Jan 2026 Governance / ML Ops
Data Contracts as GDPR: How Data Quality Hardens PII Compliance Reliability engineering and privacy compliance are usually treated as separate workstreams. They're the same discipline wearing two hats.
GDPRData ContractsGovernancePII
09 Jan 2026 Operational Efficiency
Swaps/Bike/Week: Normalising Demand to Find Fleets Under Real Stress Raw volume tells you where things are busy. A normalised metric tells you where they're about to break — and where to put your next pound of capital.
Metric DesignOperationsIoT
